person.name: Maximilian Braun
person.bio: Maximilian Braun is an Engineering Manager at SAP where he leads a team of cloud native engineers at the forefront of the digital transformation from traditional enterprise IT to modern cloud-native platforms.He was forged in the human cauldron of DevOps toil (including the genuinely terrifying Friday deployments), emerging with a perspective shaped by Infrastructure-as-Data principles and the DORA metrics ethos. He turned those hard-earned lessons into a philosophy: treat everything as code and data for full reproducibility and auditability, and let key metrics guide continuous improvement. The goal: to banish the fear from deployment days and elevate the developer experience.Today, his work extends to the broader European technology landscape. He is a workstream lead in the European IPCEI-CIS initiative and a NeoNephos Ambassador, advocating for European interoperability and an ecosystem built together on an open-source digital commons. He also champions multiple CNCF and NeoNephos projects and initiatives with his team.Looking ahead, Maximilian is focused on helping build a future where European digital sovereignty is not just a concept but a reality - powered by open, interoperable, and community-driven cloud technologies that empower the next generation of developers.

He is a startup advisor, Docker Captain, Cloud Native Ambassador, O’Reilly author, and speaker in the cloud native ecosystem.
Calcote is a maintainer of open source projects, including Meshery, Service Mesh Interface, and Service Mesh Performance. Calcote holds a bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, a master’s degree in Business Administration from California State University, Fresno and retains a list of industry certifications.

I hold both the AWS Solutions Architect Professional and DevOps Engineer Professional certifications.  I specialize in:  • Designing resilient CI/CD pipelines  • Infrastructure as Code (Terraform, Terragrunt)  • Kubernetes (I recently earned the CNCF “Kubestronaut” badge for completing all 5 Kubernetes certifications: CKA, CKAD, CKS, KCNA, KCSA)  • DevSecOps, observability, GitOps, and scalable cloud-native systems

person.name: Andrew Dean

person.name: Youssef Fathy

person.name: Kate Qiu

person.name: Jagoudel Denis

person.name: Semjon Nordmann

person.name: Kristoffer Eikså

person.name: Richard Hartmann

Go through his talks at https://github.com/RichiH/talks or follow him on Twitter at https://twitter.com/TwitchiH for musings on the intersection of technology and society.

person.name: Arthur Tira
person.bio: I'm  a software engineer with a keen interest in Cloud-native technologies. Always eager to learn, constantly seeking ways to expand my knowledge and stay at the forefront of technological advancements.

person.name: Jose Javier Acosta Santana

person.location: San Cristóbal de la Laguna, Spain

person.name: Lkhagvatuguldur Demberel

My professional journey includes working as a Senior Application Security Engineer in corporate environments, where I developed a security-first mindset that now informs my cloud and platform strategies. I thrive in the dynamic and ever-evolving Kubernetes landscape and find great satisfaction in designing scalable, resilient cloud-native systems. I'm an active supporter and learner within the CNCF community and completely grateful for the experience, thus far.

person.name: Igor Beliakov

Lately, I’ve been exploring eBPF to peek under the hood of the kernel, because observability is cooler when it feels like magic. I’m focused on bringing AI and cloud-native technologies closer together to create platforms that are smarter, adaptive, and built for the future.

person.name: Rahul Rai

I enjoy driving innovative and efficient solutions that enhance performance and empower engineering teams.

Moreover, being an ambassador provides an excellent platform for WasmEdge to engage with other projects and stay up-to-date on the latest cloud-native advancements. I can not wait to connect with other ambassadors

person.name: Roberto Tagliabue

person.name: Andrei Rusnac

In addition to the great benefit of using OSS, I strongly believe that adopting the methodology used to write, maintain and release OSS widely benefits any organization. CNCF makes cloud-native OSS accessible to everyone, and I am excited to take part in fostering the CNCF ecosystem and increasing the OSS adoption.

person.name: Michael Hajjar

Sharing my knowledges, operational excellence, customer obsession and continuous learning are my Passion.

He enjoys the rapid pace that the Cloud-Native community has been building new technologies for the future, and is excited every year to see where Cloud-Native will go next.

person.name: Roberto Gomez
person.bio: I am a Solutions Architect specializing in IT automation with VMware Aria Automation (now VCF Automation) and VMware Tanzu. Coming from the world of virtualization, and making my way as a DevOps / SRE.

person.name: Dibyajyoti Hazra

He is proficient in GoLang and has hands-on experience with containerization and orchestration technologies like Docker and Kubernetes, as well as cloud platforms such as AWS. Link to blog: https://dibyahazra.github.io/

person.name: Ahmed Doban
person.bio: I'm a Cloud Technical Lead with extensive experience designing and managing cloud solutions across AWS, GCP, and Oracle Cloud. I specialize in cloud architecture, infrastructure automation, and platform engineering, helping teams build scalable and resilient systems.

person.name: Josh Gavant

As an ambassador I'll be even more empowered to educate and support Chicago-area developers in our local communities. I'm looking forward to sharing knowledge myself, but most of all I'm looking forward to enabling people in my communities to share their knowledge with each other!

I am passionate about Kubernetes and cloud-native technologies, focusing on integration, scalability, and optimization, while staying engaged with emerging trends and best practices.

person.name: Davanum Srinivas
person.bio: Davanum Srinivas (a.k.a Dims) is a Principal Engineer with Nvidia working on Kubernetes, containerd and related projects at CNCF. Dims is involved in various Kubernetes SIGs and working groups like the Architecture, Testing, Node, Infrastructure etc.

person.name: Shijun Hou

I have worked in IT companies such as Tencent and NetEase, and have about 20 years of experience in R&D, architecture design, and operation and maintenance. I always focus on areas such as cloud native, distributed middleware, and IT architecture transformation. I am a contributor to open source software such as Kube-router, Redis-operator, Polaris, and a speaker in the cloud native community. I have participated in the formulation of more than ten cloud computing industry standards and have more than twenty technical invention patents in the field of cloud computing.

person.name: Huan Wei
person.bio: Huan has more than 15 years of work experience, and his current focus is cloud-native edge computing, distributed cloud, and high-performance computing. He is currently working as a Senior Technical Director at HarmonyCloud, a company located in Hangzhou, China, providing leading cloud-native products and solutions for hundreds of enterprise customers, including Kubernetes, PaaS, edge computing, micro service visibility, DevOps, etc. In addition, Huan serves as a Technical Steering Committe member of the CNCF edge computing project KubeEdge. Huan is an open source technology enthusiast and is currently committed to building an open source technology ecosystem in China.Why I am excited to be an ambassador: The company I work for, HarmonyCloud, is an influential cloud native tehnology supplier in China and a long-term partner of CNCF. HarmonyCloud has helped many customers around the world complete the transformation of cloud-native technology. At the same time, I am currently working as a TSC member in the CNCF Project KubeEdge community, responsible for leading several SIG teams and working with other TSC members towards the graduation of the KubeEdge project. Over the years, I have also given speeches at conferences organized by CNCF. Therefore, I am familiar with the operation process of CNCF projects and understand the job responsibilities of CNCF ambassadors. If I become an ambassador, I will promote the maturity and promotion of Kubernetes, KubeEdge and more open source projects, and help users around the world to apply these advanced and open source cloud-native tenologies.

person.name: Marco Van Kimmenaede
person.bio: I'm Marco van Kimmenaede living with my wife and daughter in Hippolytushoef. Almost 25 years experience in IT infrastructure/Linux and last year’s mostly my focus on Kubernetes and the CloudNative landscape.

person.name: David Watts
person.bio: Hi there! I'm Dave, and I'm deeply passionate about all things CNCF, especially Kubernetes. I believe true leadership isn't about rank or authority but about the choice to look after the person to the left of you and the person to the right of you. My approach to DevOps is rooted in breaking down silos, emphasizing the importance of people skills and communication to foster trust and collaboration within teams. Ensuring that everyone feels supported and empowered to succeed is what drives me.

person.name: Remco Stolwijk
person.bio: I'm a cloud-native engineer from the Netherlands. Since the start of my career, I've been working with Kubernetes and a wide range of CNCF projects. I love building scalable, modern platforms and exploring new open-source tech. You might spot me at KubeCon.

person.name: Johannes Schnatterer

Along the way, I contributed to two books (GitOps+K8s security), gave several dozen talks, wrote close to 80 articles, and trained more than 100 people in Containers, Kubernetes, and GitOps. In addition, I created open source projects like gitops-playground (GOP) and community resources like gitops-patterns, k8s-diagrams, k8s-security-demos, and most recently ckad-cka-cks-kubestronaut.  I first deployed on K8s 1.7 🙂

person.name: Tobias Lahrmann Hansen

Seeking a better way to bin-pack applications, he began advocating the use of containers; however, this didn't remove the need to hand-roll servers, so his journey led him to explore Kubernetes, where he quickly gained a taste for monitoring workloads with Prometheus and empowering his colleagues to make metrics-driven decisions.  Having worked with Kubernetes and its abstractions, Tobias still prefers his simple command line for most tasks. He firmly believes in the UNIX philosophy and KISS principles, even if daily driving Emacs might go against these principles.

I also worked as an Open Source Developer in Google Summer of Code (GSoC) at DBpedia Association. Apart from my technical experience, I got my hands dirty in fostering the Cloud Native Ecosystem. I am also the Chapter Lead at Cloud Native Hooghly which is an official CNCF Chapter listed amongst 40 such clubs in the Asia Pacific Region. Right now, with the proliferation of the Cloud Native Tools, I successfully turned the chapter to be the fastest growing in the entire Eastern India, catering 250+ Active members. I am also a Sig Contribex's Comms Volunteer for Kubernetes Contributors Summit North America 2023 at Chicago, United States of America primarily looking into the “Call for Proposal” emails and notifying about the updates to the KubeCon co-located event attendees. I have significant experience in pitching tech products to Venture Capitalists and have successfully secured grants from Government of India twice for my deep-tech startup.Why I am excited to be an ambassador: I always wanted to boost Cloud Native Ecosystem in my locality whereas I felt there is a gap of awareness. For me, every technical challenge is a designed opportunity to learn, execute, and iterate (not always in that order), a cycle that has given me powerful tools to deconstruct complex systems. This is my proven methodology for mastering what fascinates me most, a journey that has taken me through realms like GNU/Linux and BSD system administration, fullstack development, DevOps/Automation, Security and ultimately, the Kubernetes universe.  I believe technology defines us. Therefore, creating and applying it in the best possible way is, at its core, a contribution to a better society. This is why my commitment to learning is absolute and has no expiration date. It is the foundation from which I strive to reach the giants who built this wonderful open-source world, sharing whatever I can in return. Collaboration has proven to be the most viable path to the unprecedented; now is the time for all of us to contribute.

I’m a CNCF Kubestronaut (KCNA, KCSA, CKAD, CKA, CKS) and specialize in Kubernetes platform engineering, Infrastructure as Code, and cloud-native systems across AWS and Azure, plus service mesh with Istio and GitOps with Argo CD. My focus is operational excellence: SLOs/SLIs, incident response, observability (Prometheus/Grafana/ELK), and performance/cost optimization that helps teams ship reliably at scale.

person.name: Aakansha Priya

In the past, she has been a Community Manager at Hashnode & WeMakeDevs, experienced in working efficiently in groups as well as individually.  As a frontend dev turned DevOps explorer, loves sharing tech insights, and views on learning & building in public, blogging & building personal brand. Aakansha, a reader and speaker, paints, sings, and loves spontaneous travel. She has spoken at various conferences about DevOps and the human side of tech. Catch her at meetups & conferences!

person.name: Tibo Beijen
person.bio: I am an IT professional with over 20 years of experience in software engineering, architecture, and DevOps.My Kubernetes journey started in 2017. Always having leaned towards open source, I have developed a deep appreciation of the CNCF ecosystem.  Ultimately my masters in Industrial Design Engineering shines through: The tech and the focus on stakeholders and end-users, resulting in innovation and the crafting of products that offer a great experience.

person.name: Patrick Eichler

Through reading IT books, IT-Bloqs, and autodidactic studies, I constantly broaden my horizon with state-of-the-art expert knowledge and modern technical skills, which I not only use in my daily work and personal projects, but also blog about and teach to other people.

person.name: Rainui Ly
person.bio: Engineer, cloud-native computing enthusiast, open-source advocate, fan of KubeCon+CloudNativeCon. Speciality in security, software development and DevOps/Platform practices. The best process is the learning one.

person.name: Kevin (Zefeng) Wang

Kevin has contributed critical enhancements to Kubernetes, led the incubation of the KubeEdge, Volcano, Karmada projects in CNCF, actively participated in the TOC, and played a critical role in building and supporting the growth of CNCF technologies and the CNCF community in the globe.

person.name: Nikita Romm
person.bio: I'm a DevOps engineer with experience working with a wide range of tools, including Kubernetes, Terraform, multi-cloud systems, and CI/CD pipelines. I have a strong background in Linux and scripting, enabling me to automate processes and optimize workflows efficiently. My expertise extends to many other tools and technologies, and I'm constantly expanding my skill set to stay ahead in the field. I enjoy tackling complex problems and applying innovative solutions to improve system performance and efficiency. I'm committed to applying innovative solutions to every challenge and driving impactful results.

person.name: Serhii Plesovskykh
person.bio: Hello everyone, I'm a cloud architect engineer passionate about Kubernetes and cloud-native technologies. I spend 1.5 to 2 hours every day learning something new to stay sharp and grow my skills. Over the years, I've earned all certifications from AWS, GCP, and Microsoft, some of which have already been recertified for the second time, which reflects my hands-on experience with cloud infrastructure, automation, and security. I enjoy building scalable, resilient systems and constantly look for ways to improve and evolve in this fast-moving industry.

person.name: Chase Christensen
person.bio: Chase Christensen is a Principal Customer Engineer at Wiz, where he helps organizations secure cloud-native and ML workloads. His journey into MLOps began through the Kubeflow community, where he went on to chair both the 2025 Kubeflow Summit in Atlanta (https://colocatedeventsna2025.sched.com/event/28FLh/kubeflow-summit-welcome-+-opening-remarks-kubeflow-summit-a-community-big-enough-for-everyone-chase-christensen-wiz)and the Kubeflow Virtual Symposium (https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=t0vfJY267f0&list=PLj6h78yzYM2MCEJPmVgUZsEx7lFjqh8BC&index=5).  Along the way, he authored the Linux Foundation’s Introduction to AI/ML Toolkits with Kubeflow course, helping practitioners bridge Kubernetes expertise with machine learning practice. Chase also founded the Applied Machine Learning Collective of the Rockies (https://amlcoftherockies.org/), a Denver-based community focused on advancing hands-on AI/ML collaboration. He shares his thoughts on MLOps and open-source adoption as a contributor to the Evil Tux blog (https://eviltux.com/2025/06/17/the-kubeflow-blog-series/).

person.name: Jonah Kowall
person.bio: Jonah Kowall, computer scientist and open-source advocate. As a member of the founding team and technical leadership committee for OpenSearch a Linux Foundation project, Maintainer of Jaeger a CNCF graduated project for distributed tracing. Contributor to OpenTelemetry. A technical leader across startups and large enterprises specialized in operations, security, and performance. Led Gartner research on monitoring. Product leadership at AppDynamics, Cisco (post-acquisition), Kentik, Logz.io, Aiven, and is currently the Senior Vice President product and design at Paessler, the monitoring company.

person.name: Cristian Alloggio
person.bio: I am Cristian, DevOps Engineer, Solution Architect and a passionate advocate of technology innovation with a strong focus on DevOps technologies and best practices.What started as curiosity turned into passion... and now it's a mission!I am always curious to learn new technologies related to the cloud ecosystem, especially containerization.  I actively participate in the local technology community, sharing insights and experiences through collaborative projects and knowledge sharing sessions.

person.name: Jihoon Gong
person.bio: I have over 6 years of experience as a Security Compliance Engineer, with a strong ability to establish rational information protection systems that encompass development, operations, and services.

person.name: Md Touhidul Islam

Currently serving as a DevOps Lead, I have extensive expertise in Kubernetes, vSphere, Terraform, Ansible, AWS, GCP, and other cutting-edge DevOps tools, and have successfully embedded DevOps practices within teams to drive efficiency and innovation. My work in fintech has focused on enhancing security, visibility, and observability through robust monitoring, logging, and tracing solutions, ensuring scalable and secure application deployments. I am passionate about advancing cloud-native technologies and fostering best practices in the DevOps and Kubernetes ecosystems.

person.name: Preet Pal
person.bio: Cloud Engineer at Cisco

person.name: Zaynul Abedin Miah

As Bangladesh's first AWS Cloud Club to reach 1,238+ members, Zaynul has hosted global events featuring AWS leadership, trained 15+ tech professionals in cloud certifications in his study group, and organized KCD Chattogram 2024 with 2,000+ participants. Active contributor on dev.to AWS and Microsoft tag moderation, AWS Builder Center technical blogs, and recognized in AWS PartyRock Hackathon. Driven by democratizing cloud-native education across South Asia, he bridges students with global ecosystems while promoting inclusive tech communities.

person.name: Egy Ardian
person.bio: I’m a DevOps and Cloud Engineer with experience in automation, infrastructure management, and FinOps. My work spans Kubernetes operations, CI/CD engineering, and cloud cost optimization, where I’ve helped improve reliability, streamline deployments, and reduce costs. I enjoy research and innovation, mentoring teams, and leading workshops to share knowledge. Outside of work, I’m a normal guy who enjoys learning new technologies and experimenting with creative cloud-native solutions. also, I Love Cats.

person.name: Jason Khoi
person.bio: I am a Senior DevOps Engineer transitioned from more than a decade old of software developer role. As a result, I know Cloud infrastructure and software development equally well. I love the philosophy of Kubernetes and have a passion of using CNCF Prometheus for infrastructure and application observability & monitoring.

person.name: Nguyen Doan
person.bio: Hello, greetings to those who are passionate about cloud-native technologies. Like everyone else, I really enjoy exploring and delving into new things within CNCF tools, then applying these insights to my company and sharing them with others.

person.name: Amr Muhammed Mahmoud
person.bio: Enthusiastic DevOps/Platform Engineer dedicated to delivering innovative and scalable solutions that drive organizational growth. With expertise in CI/CD pipeline architecture, Kubernetes management, cloud-native infrastructure, observability, and security integration, I ensure secure and efficient software delivery. I leverage cutting-edge technologies, particularly within the Kubernetes and CNCF ecosystems, to drive operational excellence and foster innovation. Passionate about collaborating with highly skilled teams to lead impactful digital transformation initiatives, bridging the gap between development and operations to achieve efficiency, resilience, and continuous improvement.

person.name: João Santos
person.bio: I’m a DevSecOps Engineer at Scalabit.dev, where I help teams ship software securely and efficiently through automation, CI/CD, IaC, Kubernetes, and Security best practices.  In 2023, I transitioned from a 7-year career as a personal trainer to tech — with no prior experience and a tight schedule (full-time job + wife + 2 kids!). I did it all using free resources, discipline, and determination.  Now, I’m passionate about helping others make the same leap into tech — especially those seeking flexibility, growth, and better work-life balance. 💻🌍 👉 ebook.jocasantos.com

person.name: Liz Rice
person.bio: Liz Rice is Chief Open Source Officer with eBPF specialists Isovalent, now part of Cisco, and original creators of the Cilium cloud native networking, security and observability project. She is the author of Container Security, and Learning eBPF, both published by O'Reilly. She was Chair of the CNCF's Technical Oversight Committee in 2019-2022, and Co-Chair of KubeCon + CloudNativeCon in 2018.She has a wealth of software development, team, and product management experience from working on network protocols and distributed systems, and in digital technology sectors such as VOD, music, and VoIP. When not writing code, or talking about it, Liz loves riding bikes in places with better weather than her native London, competing in virtual races on Zwift, and making music under the pseudonym Insider Nine.

person.name: Patryk Wieczorek
person.bio: I am passionate about IT and I run the most dedicated study group and stream daily how I study at 5 AM at https://www.youtube.com/@thefiveamstudy

person.name: Ebin Babu
person.bio: I'm Ebin Babu, an engineer turned event organizer on a mission to ignite the cloud-native revolution in Kerala. As a passionate evangelist in the open-source world, I juggle multiple roles – from heading operations at Stackgenie.io to co-founding devopsmalayalam.io and spearheading the CNCG Trivandrum chapter. My overarching aim? To cultivate a vibrant, collaborative cloud-native community in our dynamic state.  Throughout my journey as an organizer, I've orchestrated over 100 online and offline events, fostering connections and exchanging knowledge among fellow cloud-native enthusiasts. It's been incredibly rewarding to witness this community flourish and expand. Together, we're nurturing a shared passion for open-source, Linux, Kubernetes, and the ever-evolving world of Istio.  What truly fuels my drive is the diverse tapestry of minds within our community – constantly pushing boundaries and shaping the future of technology. I derive immense satisfaction from uniting these innovators and providing a platform for collaboration, ideation, and collective growth.

person.name: Wouter Damman
person.bio: Hi! I’m Wouter, a Cloud Engineer from the Netherlands with a passion for Kubernetes and the cloud-native world. My interest in technology and automation led me down this path, allowing me to explore how the cloud-native world fits together. both at work and in my homelab. I enjoy solving problems through automation and turning ideas into reliable, maintainable solutions.

person.name: Matthew Robinson
person.bio: Matt has been doing DevOps since the mid-90's and for more than twenty years has been working in the higher education sector, ensuring students and researchers have access to the resources they need. Matt was introduced to containers almost a decade ago and a cloud migration project in 2000 allowed a move to Kubernetes. Kubernetes allowed a complex migration to be completed quickly and painlessly. As new challenges need solving, he often finds the solution exists in another CNCF project (Argo, Helm, Prometheus, OPA etc). Matt is excited about the future of cloud native and the future of CNCF projects.

person.name: Mohsin Nisar
person.bio: Experienced professional with expertise in Kubernetes, DevOps, MLOps, and cloud technologies. Skilled in designing scalable, automated solutions to optimize workflows, boost operational efficiency, and foster innovation in cloud-native ecosystems.

person.name: Gary Chang

As a dedicated Kubestronaut, I am passionate about orchestrating containerized microservices and implementing GitOps workflows with Argo CD and Terraform to build resilient cloud-native platforms. I thrive on automating complex self-service pipelines and am always eager to experiment with new CNCF projects to solve real-world scaling challenges.

person.name: Raymond Tan
person.bio: As a cloud-native architect, I'm driven to build resilient and lasting systems by blending timeless architectural principles with pragmatic, automated operations. I have a discerning eye for quality design, always aiming for solutions that are as scalable and performant as they are simple to maintain and evolve. Ultimately, my philosophy is to pursue an elegant efficiency, crafting distributed systems that empower developers and deliver enduring value to the community.

person.name: Sarawut Klangphukhiao

I'm an active contributor in local DevOps and cloud communities in Thailand, and I enjoy mentoring others preparing for CNCF certifications.

person.name: Venkatesh Venugopal
person.bio: A seasoned Senior DevOps Engineer with 15 years of experience building the backbone of mission-critical systems. My expertise lies in transforming complex cloud-native ecosystems into high-performance, secure, and cost-efficient engines of growth. Beyond mastering Kubernetes orchestration and CI/CD automation, I am dedicated to the art of SRE—ensuring system reliability while driving the continuous improvement that keeps modern enterprises ahead of the curve. I believe in leading by example, mentoring teams, and architecting the future of scalable infrastructure.

person.name: Matthew Rodgers
person.bio: I am a Senior Software Engineer with a passion for cloud-native technologies and the Kubernetes ecosystem. I have experience developing modern applications, migrating legacy systems, and deploying tools that enhance workflows within Kubernetes environments. I enjoy solving complex challenges around scalability and reliability while writing clean, maintainable code. Excited to keep learning, I look forward to deepening my expertise and contributing to the Kubernetes community.

person.name: Omar Elfarsaoui
person.bio: I’m Omar, a passionate cloud-native enthusiast with a strong love for Kubernetes, DevOps culture, and security. I enjoy building, breaking, and securing systems while continuously learning through hands-on projects, books, and the open-source community.  I code for fun, automate everything I can, and I’m deeply interested in how infrastructure, security, and software intersect in modern cloud environments.  Currently focused on:  Kubernetes & Cloud Native ecosystems DevOps & Platform Engineering Infrastructure as Code Cloud & Container Security

person.name: David Gondor
person.bio: I'm David Gondor, a Test engineer with a strong focus on quality and reliability. I'm transitioning into DevOps, expanding my skills in automation, cloud, and CI/CD. I’m passionate about building efficient, scalable systems that support fast and reliable software delivery.
person.location: Alphen aan den Rijn, Zuid-Holland, Netherlands

person.name: Hyungchan Cho
person.bio: Hello, I’m currently leading ALM and DevOps engineering initiatives at Hyundai Mobis, where I focus on driving innovation in areas such as CI/CD and cloud-native transformation.  For over three years, my group and I have designed, built, and operated a hybrid cloud infrastructure that seamlessly integrates an on-premises Kubernetes cluster with Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS) — achieving an optimal balance of scalability and cost efficiency.  My core expertise lies in Kubernetes operations, security, and observability, ensuring resilient and transparent platform management across complex environments.  I’m also deeply passionate about Generative AI technologies, particularly AI-powered code assistants, Model Context Protocol (MCP), and Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) — exploring how these can further elevate developer productivity and system intelligence.

person.name: Joel G.
person.bio: I love to develop secure, reliable and scalable services while automating redundant tasks to keep my productivity high. I really enjoy learning new things to keep up with the future and deepen my knowledge to a level where I am able to teach about it.

person.name: Marcus Ross
person.bio: In the past ten years, I have been addicted to the cloud (AWS/Azure/OpenTofu) and Kubernetes, helping enterprise customers to achieve their solutions. Always try to align business requirements with technology. I share my real-world experience from projects as an official Linuxfoudnation Kubernetes Trainer, AWS ChampionTrainer, and MCT.

person.name: Uroš Hercog
person.bio: I'm a Site Reliability Engineer at Veza, the Identity Security company, primarily focusing on tools like Kubernetes, Prometheus, and Cilium. My expertise lies in optimizing infrastructure resilience and automating deployment processes to improve reliability across dynamic environments.

person.name: Adhi Sutandi
person.bio: Senior DevOps Engineer based in Zürich, specializing in helping organizations scale their IT systems and achieve N times developer productivity. I focus on building solutions that directly fit business goals and are designed to last, based on solid system designs. My toolkit includes key CNCF technologies like Kubernetes, Flux, Helm, Prometheus, and provisioning tools like OpenTofu and Sops. I share my occasional insights and technical guides on my blog: https://medium.com/@sanadhis.

person.name: Jacopo Binosi
person.bio: I first encountered Linux at university, and from that moment, I never looked back. It was then that I realized Linux would be my lifelong "familiar."  I enjoy solving problems and making things work the way I want. This, combined with my passion for Linux, led me to a career as a Systems Engineer after earning my Bachelor's degree in Physics. Time flew by, and I transitioned to the Cloud... and to Neovim (I’ve always been a daily Vim user and a fan of CLI tools in general).  Today, I’m deeply into Kubernetes, but I still use Vim, Ubuntu, Debian, Arch, and occasionally work with some legacy infrastructure. Now that I’m a "Kubestronaut," I see this as a fresh starting point, without forgetting the tools that got me here.

person.name: Thibault Sourdin
person.bio: I’m a cloud engineer with 2 years of production experience on Kubernetes. My background includes embedded systems (building embedded Linux distributions), which shaped my interest in understanding systems beneath their abstractions. I enjoy diving into Kubernetes internals, Linux, networking, and security to design reliable infrastructures.

person.name: Anis Hannachi
person.bio: Platform & DevOps engineer with strong hands-on experience operating Kubernetes platforms using Helm and GitOps with Argo CD. I work across the full DevOps stack, from cluster operations and platform architecture to CI/CD, automation and observability. My background spans on-premise, hybrid, and cloud environments, where I focus on building scalable and maintainable Kubernetes-based systems. I enjoy designing platform architectures, automating workflows, and enabling teams to run production workloads efficiently.

person.name: Peter West
person.bio: I've worked in the IT industry for 35 years and have held a variety of roles during this time.  More recently i've worked extensively with Kubernetes and AWS whilst automating using languages such as Python and Go.  Although I don't currently use Kubernetes as part of my day to day work I find it incredibly interesting so keep my hand in by running clusters at home which are then used for various projects.
person.location: Cleethorpes, North East Lincolnshire, United Kingdom

person.name: Phoo Phoo Thet Lyar Tun

I attended KubeDay Singapore 2023, staying up to date with the latest trends in cloud-native computing.

person.name: Than Htike Phyo

I work extensively with cloud-native and Kubernetes environments, focusing on CNCF technologies such as Kubernetes, service mesh, and cloud security integration. My experience spans secure campus networking, micro-segmentation, and integrating security into DevOps and container platforms. I am passionate about bridging traditional network security with modern cloud-native architectures. I regularly share insights and technical deep dives on infrastructure security and Kubernetes security best practices.

person.name: Momoko Murakami
person.bio: I am a Cloud Security Engineer specializing in designing, implementing, and operating security solutions for multi-cloud environments, including AWS, Google Cloud, and Kubernetes. I also leverage Infrastructure as Code (IaC) for secure, automated deployments.

person.name: Tony Prawiro Hadimulyono

In his job, Tony collaborates with others to create threat models, suggest security controls, and develop security monitoring use cases for SIEM systems.  Tony enjoys learning new things and has earned certifications from AWS, Microsoft Azure, Google Cloud, ISC2 CSSLP, and CNCF/Linux Foundation Kubernetes. He is now learning about Machine Learning and Generative AI, with the goal of using AI to enhance the security of cloud and DevOps platforms.

person.name: Woravit Ariyakunatorn
person.bio: With over a decade of experience in IT, I specialize in cloud architecture, DevOps, and automation. I enjoy experimenting with Kubernetes and other cloud-native technologies both professionally and as a hobby. My interests include designing secure, scalable infrastructure and building modern solutions using AWS, Kubernetes, and serverless platforms. Outside of technology, I’m passionate about fishing and exploring the outdoors.

Supporting a well-established client by provisioning platform information systems, enabling Kubernetes-based workloads for seamless communication and operational efficiency. Beyond cloud-native engineering, I'm actively exploring Generative AI, continuously expanding my skillset to stay ahead in the evolving tech landscape. Pursuing industry-recognised certifications to solidify my expertise and stand out in a competitive field. Please follow me on https://www.linkedin.com/in/amitdctm/, https://github.com/amitdctm and https://gitlab.com/documentum-stack

person.name: Rajan Jha

Bringing 10+ years of experience, I specialise in Platform Engineering, DevOps, and Site Reliability Engineering (SRE), with a proven track record of designing, building, and managing resilient, scalable, and secure infrastructure and services. Starting my journey as Linux Systems Engineering, I gained a strong foundation in application lifecycles and modern IT Infrastructure management, enhancing my expertise in modern platform solutions.

person.name: Halil Ibrahim Bugol
person.bio: I started my career in a data center in Istanbul, Turkey. I have been interested in the CNCF world since 2018. Although my main areas are generally technical aspects, there were companies where I took part in community and product management. I am currently in the role of Co-Founder at Kubezy. Apart from the commercial side, we have been organizing events as Organizers at CNCG Istanbul since last year, together with my friends in Turkey. I am also a member of the KCD Türkiye team. I have a community role as Technology Evangelist at KubeSphere and KubeSphere Products.Why I am excited to be an ambassador: I am in love with community and all things community. I have met so many people since I first joined the CNCF community. It is a great pleasure to contribute to this community with people from all over the world who believe in the same goal. One goal and hundreds of people contributing to this goal. I think my biggest excitement in being an ambassador is making new friends and representing my community in the best way possible. Also, ambassador swags are great :)

person.name: Jay Koby
person.bio: Jay Koby brings more than a decade of experience working with a variety of organizations and technologies.  He currently works as a Senior Observability Architect at Grafana Labs.

person.name: Long Li
person.bio: I am an SRE. I began exploring cloud-native technologies in 2022 and successfully obtained CKA and CKS certifications. I have developed a strong passion for cloud-native technologies and am currently focusing on cloud-native DevOps practices.

person.name: Chimbu Chinnadurai
person.bio: Chimbu is a passionate senior cloud architect with a knack for solving complex cloud challenges.He has a proven track record of helping companies of all sizes leverage the power of Kubernetes and achieve greater scalability, agility, and resilience.He actively shares his expertise through insightful blog posts on his Medium page (https://chimbu.medium.com/) and by engaging with the Open Source and Cloud Native communities at industry conferences.

person.name: Roberth Strand
person.bio: I am a self-proclaimed "cloud automator", and I work at Sopra Steria based out of Oslo, Norway. I have been granted the title of Microsoft MVP for my work with Azure, and I have been an active contributor in the CNCF for which I earned the title Cloud Native Ambassador. I initiated Cloud Native Norway, a non-profit that works to build up and enable Cloud Native adoption and development in Norway. I also co-founded the Azure Cloud Native User Group, and founded the Norwegian PowerShell User Group. In the CNCF, I have been active for years in TAG App Delivery, and currently serves as co-chair for the TAG, maintainer on the OpenGitOps project, and as co-chair of the Platforms Working Group.Why I am excited to be an ambassador: For many years, the CNCF has continued to impress me. The way that the foundation has supported the community and the projects is truly admirable. Even though I have other honorary titles, the CNCF Ambassador title is one that I value very much. While I don't do community work for the titles, I am happy for the recognition. I have big plans for my work in the CNCF, and I look forward to seeing how the ambassador program will help me achieve more for the community.Sessionize: https://sessionize.com/roberth-strand

person.name: Łukasz Amróz
person.bio: Frontend Developer that have been working with Kubernetes for the past three years. Interested in DevOps tools and technologies.

person.name: Artem Bortnikov
person.bio: Passionate software/infrastructure engineer. Keen on Kubernetes. In love with Golang and Rust. I love being on the cutting edge of technology and bringing things together that weren't originally meant to be together.

person.name: Kylash Mukund Rameshbapu
person.bio: I am currently working as a DevOps Engineer with over 4 years of combined experience in managing ETL workloads and deploying cloud applications using DevOps practices. Recently, I transitioned into the DevOps domain, where I have built hands-on expertise working with various Devops tools. Having the Kubestronaut credential demonstrates my commitment to continuous learning and my ability to adapt to new technologies. I am excited to leverage my skills and contribute to the Cloud Native community in the coming years.

person.name: Matteo Mortari
person.bio: I strongly believe Kubernetes is an awesome platform not only for cloud-native, app orchestration and workload, but especially for MLOps (Kubeflow) and in Containerization: not only for runnable images but also for broad OCI packaging and distribution (ORAS/Podman/Buildah) especially in relation to ML model assets and integration with signature, attestation. I contribute to Kubeflow, Oras, and other Open Source projects. I'm a co-organizer of the local JUG (JVM User Group) Milano, and I'm active in other Italian communities, often contributing my passion/hobby of live streaming and video recording.

person.name: Roland Rust
person.bio: With a background in software development and system architecture, I understand the importance of reliable and scalable infrastructure platforms. As a Site Reliability Engineer, I focus on building and operating highly available hybrid cloud environments. My expertise includes managing multiple Kubernetes clusters and diverse infrastructure components using infrastructure-as-code practices. I am dedicated to empowering development teams through efficient GitOps workflows and am passionate about monitoring and metrics to ensure optimal system performance and resiliency. I strive to bridge the gap between development and operations, driving continuous improvement across the organization.

person.name: Bob Killen
person.bio: Bob Killen is a Senior Technical Program Manager focusing on improving CNCF project governance processes and finding engaging ways to bring end users and maintainers together. He serves the Kubernetes project as a Steering Committee member and is a recently retired Contributor Experience Special Interest Group chair. Before joining the CNCF, Bob was a program manager at Google's Open Source Programs Office, where he guided and developed strategies for Google's open source projects. Bob has a history of bringing cloud and HPC/AI/ML together and is passionate about Academic Outreach. He is also an outspoken advocate of Open Source, Open Science, and pets of the furry 4-legged variety.

person.name: Viktar Mikalayeu
person.bio: SRE  , AWS solution architect , FinOps consultant,DevOps  , AWS Community Builder , Open-source

person.name: Igor Eulalio
person.bio: Container, Cloud and Kubernetes expert, organizer of KCD Brasil.Currently main contributor of Falco Talon, a project from the Falco ecosystem, aiming to provide open source automatic threat response for Kubernetes.

person.name: Katherine Hughes
person.bio: Katherine brings over a decade of experience in event management, with a strong background in planning and executing corporate and nonprofit conferences and events of all sizes across a wide range of industries and destinations. She has a proven track record in contract negotiation, event logistics, and production management. Detail-oriented and creativity-driven, Katherine thrives on juggling complex logistics, solving problems in real time, and collaborating with cross-functional teams to deliver seamless, high-impact experiences. She’s always looking for ways to improve efficiency, elevate attendee engagement, and exceed expectations.

person.name: Arjun Kaushal
person.bio: Cloud consultant with 5+ years of experience migrating applications from on-prem to the cloud. Strong proficiency in Azure, and learned Kubernetes through multiple projects which took advantage of AKS. Passionate about DevOps, IaC, and Security, and always excited to learn more.

person.name: Fangel Emilio Colón Navarro
person.bio: Hello, I'm Fangel, I live in the Dominican Republic. My first steps using CNCF technologies began in 2020 when the technological world advanced significantly. As of today I have already achieved a lot of knowledge and my wishes are to continue in this wonderful world.

person.name: Karthi Palanisamy

He actively contributes to the Kubernetes ecosystem, including documentation contributions to the Kubernetes website and regular participation in SIG ContribEx meetings, with a growing focus on community enablement and contributor experience. His technical expertise spans Kubernetes operations, GitOps, Infrastructure as Code, and reliability automation, with real-world impact in platform governance and cloud cost optimization. Karthi is also an open-source contributor and writes about SRE and cloud-native operations on Medium (https://medium.com/@karthipalanisamy14).  His current interests include applying AI to improve Kubernetes reliability, SRE workflows, and operational decision-making in complex systems.

person.name: Kostiantyn Lysenko

Kenta Tada has worked as a Linux engineer and team lead for various organizations, specializing in Linux kernel tracing technologies and eBPF. He has also contributed to several CNCF-related projects, including serving as a reviewer for the system validator used in kubeadm preflight checks.

In 2023, he reviewed the Japanese translation of Learning eBPF, published by O'Reilly Japan. In 2024, he became an organizer of Cloud Native Community Japan (CNCJ), fostering open source culture in Japan. Additionally, he served as Co-Chair of KubeDay Japan 2024, playing a key role in shaping the event and promoting cloud-native technologies within the Japanese developer community.

Recognizing the growing importance of eBPF in cloud-native environments, Kenta founded the eBPF Japan Community for engineers across various industries to collaborate, share knowledge, and drive eBPF adoption in Japan.

With over 15 years of open source leadership, Jonathan co-founded the OpenStack project, guiding its evolution into a global ecosystem that supports a multi-billion-dollar market and serves as the de facto standard for open cloud infrastructure. Before his open source career, Jonathan spent over a decade in data center infrastructure and co-founded one of the industry's earliest cloud computing companies, which grew into a billion-dollar business following its acquisition by Rackspace. Passionate about open collaboration, infrastructure innovation, and empowering users, contributors, and ecosystems worldwide, Jonathan frequently speaks at international conferences on open source strategy, infrastructure modernization, cloud-native technologies, and open source AI initiatives.

software defined network, and high availability etc.

under CNCF, Linux Foundation Networking, Linux Foundation Edge and Open Infrastructure.
She has made strong contributions to the technical direction and design, driving working group
discussion, and resolving technical disagreements. She co-authored the CNCF serverless
whitepaper, the CloudEvents specification, and the OpenStack service function chaining design.
She has served as the program committee member/track chair for several Kubecons and

As an Ambassador, I will contribute to thought leadership in the cloud native space and hope to provide an instrumental role in fostering a culture of knowledge sharing, learning and innovation. Throughout this journey, I am also eager to support and encourage others in pursuing CNCF Cloud Native certifications - an endeavour I am both passionate about and an advocate for. Passionate about the magic of Kubernetes and the incredible CNCF ecosystem, I designed, developed, and implemented Natura's internal development platform, now called Amazonia, from scratch. Currently, this platform is responsible to manage all the cloud-native infrastructure for all countries and brands of Natura Group. The Amazonia platform has transformed teams' experience managing their product infrastructure, improved collaboration and efficiency, reduced cognitive load, and shortened the Natura Engineering Team's overall time to market. As a result of this transformation in our operations, we won the DevOps Dozen 2022 award in the Best DevOps Transformation (Non-Vendor) category. As a GitOps enthusiast, I also implemented a fully automated Git-based operational model with ArgoCD across our 40+ Kubernetes clusters. This brought us traceability, reduced error occurrence, and enabled us to have a functioning DR environment, thus increasing the operational excellence of our cloud engineering team.  KCD Brazil speaker and re:Invent attendee.

person.name: Daniel D M
person.bio: Meet a Cloud Architect who writes code with the enthusiasm of a kid in a candy store. Armed with coffee and an insatiable appetite for automation, I transform mundane tasks into elegant solutions while Kubernetes keeps me humble with its delightful complexities.  As a devoted servant of Jesus Christ, I approach technology with the same dedication as my faith. When not wrestling with cloud infrastructure, you'll find me exploring new technologies and tools, always curious to learn what's next in the tech world. I embrace a minimalist lifestyle, finding joy in simplicity both in code and in life.  My love affair with Kubernetes stems from its beautiful chaos - where community-driven solutions tackle real-world problems head-on. Like a digital shepherd, I guide applications through the cloud, learning and growing alongside an amazing community of problem solvers.  Think of me as your friendly neighbourhood automation enthusiast, fueled by coffee and powered by faith, always ready to transform complex challenges into elegant solutions. Because in the end, technology is not just about code - it's about building bridges and growing together.  Life's journey isn't just about conquering technical challenges; it's about leaving footprints of kindness and knowledge for others to follow. After all, the greatest legacy we can leave behind is not in the systems we build, but in the lives we touch through sharing and caring.  Personal Blog: https://bookofdaniel.in/

person.name: Constanze Roedig
person.bio: Key Researcher & Lecturer, Public Speaker, Creator of the Software Bill of Behavior (SBoB) and maintainer of the Kubernetes Storm Center for cloudnative intelligence Backstory: Living intentionally is hard when routine takes control. I grew up wanting to be a scientist, studying Biochemistry, starting med school, and ultimately earning a PhD in General Relativity to explore how humans perceive 'reality.' When US funding cuts ended that path, I realized academia left me without agency.In the private sector, I discovered the value of risk. The Cloud Native paradigm let me experiment, fail fast, and try again without fear of lasting damage. In positive-sum collaborations, I saw how my algorithms could shape real-world outcomes, and how taking calculated risks created visible change for people and systems alike. This freedom to test ideas with relatively low cost revealed a new kind of agency, an immediacy and measurable connection to the meaning of work.My vision is to empower others to leverage cloud native technology with respect for the human element, security, and risk. Because having agency means creating reality for our society, for each one of us, one epsilon at a time.

If you ask me the question, “Which one do you prefer?” I’d answer that it depends on your use case!  Since 2024, I’ve been an Ambassador for the Silicon Chalet. This Meetup brings together a community of over 1,600 members from Switzerland and France! My role is to promote tools and approaches close to my heart, either by organising events or by presenting topics.

person.name: Sébastien Davoult
person.bio: Passionate about Virtualization, Containerization and Observability technologies, I have made it my job. Today, I accompany our customers in their #MoveToCloud transformation mainly on OnPremise environments by maximizing the use of OpenSource products. Much more than just products, OpenSource is for me a philosophy. I love to share and transmit on the subjects which impassion me, I do it with students, conferences, and also by publishing specialized blogs posts.Why I am excited to be an ambassador: I am very happy and excited to have joined this program. It will allow me to promote the Cloud Native more widely in France. It's a subject that is still unknown in some companies and the specialized content is often not translated from English. My goal is to promote the power of the Cloud Native through French articles, Meetups, and conferences.

person.name: Tim Berry

person.name: Amro Elamin
person.bio: A cloud architect that’s enthusiastic about container orchestration and open source cloud-native technologies.

person.name: Bosch Luckas

person.location: New york, New york, United States

person.name: Karthik Kota

person.name: David Lautie

person.name: Eric Ng

person.name: Wolfram Gerret Apelt

person.name: Naresh Duddu

Naresh holds the position of Associate Vice President and Global Head of Modernization Practice at Infosys. In his role, he oversees the development of intellectual property (IP), collaboration with modernization partners, and introduction of new services within the domain of application modernization. The practice led by Naresh takes a prominent role in spearheading application transformation initiatives across diverse industries and service lines. It places particular emphasis on four fundamental pillars of application modernization: Open Source, Agile/DevOps, Legacy modernization, and Cloud.

Over the course of his 29-year career, Naresh has occupied various roles that have encompassed IT services delivery, program management, enterprise architecture, as well as incubating and fostering multiple specialized groups.

Naresh holds a B Tech in Metallurgical Engineering from Indian Institute of Technology (BHU) Varanasi, India.

Beyond consulting, I am deeply committed to sharing knowledge as both a Linux Foundation Authorized Instructor and a Red Hat Certified Architect and Instructor. This allows me to bridge the gap between high-level theory and hands-on implementation, helping engineers and organizations master the tools necessary for modern cloud environments.

person.name: Maksim Burakouski

person.name: Vishal Kohli

person.name: Shabbir Saifee

person.name: Tom Chrulski

Above all, I love spending time with my two sons—whether we’re playing, exploring, or just being silly together. When I get a moment to myself, you’ll find me at the piano, over a game of chess, or solving Hanayama puzzles. BTW. I use NixOS—because reproducibility matters, in systems and in life.

person.name: Jayesh Pal

person.name: Rohit Ghumare

Besides penning down his knowledge, Rohit is dedicated to sharing his insights through the "Cloud Native Talks" podcast, which he hosts, and through global conferences and local meetups where he's presented multiple talks. He has also been a program committee member for Kubecon co-located events. Links: devopsguys.inWhy I am excited to be an ambassador: Being appointed as an ambassador presents a remarkable opportunity to further extend the outreach of my engagement within the tech community, particularly in the realms of DevOps and Cloud Native technologies. It will help me to give back to the community by sharing insights from experts located all over the world.

person.name: Santosh Nukala
person.bio: Eagerly pursues knowledge in the ever-evolving field of CNCF technologies ,driven by deep-seated curiosity to innovate and optimize cloud and container security practices.

person.name: Melcolm Lee

person.name: Cornelis Stuurman

person.name: Tan Kar Chun

person.name: Alolita Sharma

Alolita is an OpenTelemetry GC member, CNCF Observability TAG co-chair and CNCF Governing Board alternate board member from Apple. She leads Apple’s AIML observability practice and contributes to open source and open standards in OpenTelemetry, Unicode, W3C and the Observability TAG.

As a founding member of the CNCF’s End User TAB, Alolita has facilitated participation and contributions by CNCF end user members. She has also promoted end user priorities to the CNCF TOC and Governing Board. Within her rich track record, Alolita has demonstrated a deep commitment to CNCF's goals and initiatives. Alolita brings a wealth of experience from her board leadership roles in the industry. For example, she has served on the boards of the OSI, SFLC.in and Unicode Consortium. Alolita has also provided strong leadership for observability and search engineering at AWS and has managed engineering teams at IBM, PayPal, Twitter, and Wikipedia.

person.name: Victor Caceres Chian
person.bio: Data Engineer focused on building scalable data platforms, ETL pipelines, and CI/CD workflows in cloud-native environments. Interested in Kubernetes and platform engineering, with a continuous learning mindset.

person.name: Cristher Castro
person.bio: Cristher Castro, co-founder and COO of Cuemby, is a visionary leader and operational dynamo who drives community building in the Latam tech scene. Over the last 3 years, she advocated for the adoption of open-source technologies within the Spanish-speaking community, creating and supporting tech meetups and events in the region. She co-created, along with her team, the first Kubernetes Community Days in Colombia. This event paved the way for the expansion of Cloud Native Groups throughout the region. Cristher's foresight and operational expertise have been fundamental to the success of both Cuemby and the Cloud Native Latam Group. Recognizing the region's need for in-depth knowledge of cloud-native applications, Cristher championed the LFS250 (Linux Foundation Course) and administered the KCNA certifications donated by CNCF to the region. These two initiatives heightened awareness of cloud-native technologies and certifications, fortifying local proficiency in open-source solutions. She continues to promote and advocate for the recognition of the Cloud Native Computing Foundation (CNCF) in the region, addressing both business and community aspects. Cristher's leadership and operational prowess exemplify her unwavering commitment to fostering a thriving Latam tech community. https://community.cncf.io/cloud-native-latin-america/ https://community.cncf.io/cloud-native-medellin/ https://www.cncf.io/reports/kcd-colombia-2023/ https://www.cuemby.com/about-us https://www.linkedin.com/company/fundaci%C3%B3n-hispana-de-cloud-native/?viewAsMember=true https://community.cncf.io/cloud-native-colombia/ https://www.linkedin.com/showcase/kcd-colombia/?viewAsMember=truehttps://www.linkedin.com/showcase/kcd-colombiaWhy I am excited to be an ambassador: Becoming a CNCF ambassador empowers me to further my dedication to community growth in the LATAM region. I deeply believe that the region's success hinges on the symbiotic collaboration of professionals, students, and companies. It's this harmony that forms the core of my mission: to cultivate a thriving community ecosystem centered around open-source and cloud-native technologies. Through this role, I am positioned to amplify awareness about these technologies and the transformative opportunities they introduce. My operational and logistics expertise is set to facilitate more meetups and events, thus kindling the formation of new communities. Collaborating with fellow leaders and ambassadors provides a unique platform to unite our efforts, ensuring we collectively advance our shared vision.

She has also been a featured speaker at the Open Source Summit, where she shared her insights on the intersection of open source and community engagement.Since 2022, Audra has served as a Community and Outreach Manager at the Cloud Native Computing Foundation (CNCF), where she drives global initiatives like Kubernetes Community Days (KCDs), Cloud Native Community Groups (CNCGs), the ChatLoopBackOff (CLBO) livestream series, and CNCF’s member video and streaming programs.Audra is fueled by a commitment to elevate emerging voices and deepen community engagement. Her passion lies in connecting industry leaders, spotlighting technical innovators, and creating platforms where ideas spark, grow, and resonate globally.

You can occasionally find him posting to his blog (https://sagikazarmark.hu/), but chances are his GitHub profile is more interesting. In his free time, Márk likes riding a bicycle, reading books, but his favorite hobby for more than 20 years has been Hungarian folk dance.Why I am excited to be an ambassador: I always liked learning/building new technologies and then sharing the knowledge with others. By working on various Open Source projects for more than a decade I also learned to appreciate the opportunity to learn from people coming from different backgrounds. By being an ambassador I hope that I'll have more opportunities to connect with people, learn from them and maybe share some of my knowledge as well.

person.name: Konrad Rzentarzewski
person.bio: I was there when the config burned. In the age before observability, chaos reigned. The servers howled, and nothing scaled but entropy. Then kube cattle were born — simple, nameless beasts born of children’s games. But they multiplied. And their hunger grew vast. Years passed. The sun revolved the Earth. Then the Earth revolved the Cloud. I grew tired of debugging in darkness. I longed for light. And logs. Preferably structured. That’s when I began preaching — not loudly, just… declaratively. One apply at a time. I walked among DevOps tribes, where bash begat shell, and shell begat pain. To them I brought ConfigMaps, and Secrets hidden in plain sight. Then came the mysterious YAML — a language too holy to lint — inscribed deep within the API’s own heart. Through toil and merge, the infrastructure became manifest. Pods floated on the waters. Deployments begat replicas. Helm bore Charts. Git begat Ops. What was once dev spun to prod. And every service had a name — and every name resolved. Unless external DNS was involved. And the Preachers said: \"Behold, our logs streameth clean.\" Thus began the age of the declarative. We once dwelled in darkness. Now, we scale planetary in full light.

He is a Co-organizer of the Cloud Native Meetups in Bulgaria since 2018, where the community has grown from 20 to over 2000 members, and a Co-organizer of Kubernetes Community Days Sofia. He speaks at meetups and actively mentors people, helping them gain the confidence to step on stage and share their knowledge. His focus areas include Kubernetes at scale, SRE, Cloud Native, Observability, and Cost Optimization. He strongly believes in the power of community and the idea that sharing knowledge can change the way people think and work. Based in Sofia, Bulgaria, Spas enjoys baking sourdough bread, making barbecues, and cooking huge stacks of pancakes – but most of all, spending time with his family. He is also known for his favorite question: "Why?". Why he is motivated: "I love seeing the spark of satisfaction in people's eyes when I have helped them learn something or see things from a different perspective."

person.name: Paolo Patierno
person.bio: I am a Senior Principal Software Engineer working at IBM on messaging and data streaming technologies. I am also a committer on a number of IoT related projects and interested in observability and metrics – as a side project I look at how Formula 1 telemetry can be analysed with the technologies I work with every day. I am based in Napoli, where I am the lead organiser of the local CNCF Meetup.Why I am excited to be an ambassador: I feel that I am one of the lucky people who love their job because it's also their passion. Taking this passion to the community by sharing my knowledge about open source technologies is something which excites me. To be a CNCF Ambassador takes this love to the next level, being even more engaged within the cloud native space.

person.name: Roby Chen
person.bio: I am honored to continue serving in this role. Over the past few years, My company DaoCloud has made continuous contributions to the open-source community. Our contributions to Kubernetes, a core cloud-native project, have ranked among the top three globally over the past year. In 2022, we will also take on a contributing seat on Istio's steering committee. We have independently open-sourced nine projects, including Clusterpedia, Merbridge and Piraeus which have successfully entered CNCF Sandbox incubation. DaoCloud continues to contribute significantly towards cloud-native technology and improve CNCF's technical ecosystem.Why I am excited to be an ambassador: Firstly, I hope that cloud-native technology will remain highly connected to China, and that the integration of the technology world will not be affected by geography, politics, international conflicts and other factors. Secondly, I hope that cloud-native technology can break through the barriers between technology and users, and that more end-user voices can be fed back to CNCF. Thirdly, I hope to promote the building of cloud-native technology talent and further improve the cloud-native education system, so that CNCF will become a sustainable organization.

Being an ambassador will give me more opportunities to connect with other cloud native enthusiasts and learn from their experiences. I hope to inspire more people to join the cloud native journey and make a positive impact on the world.

The cloud native world and its vast eco-system is evolving at high pace and today's challenge for many companies is to make sure they're not outrun by this strong wind of change in the digital transformation race. I aim to bridge the gap between hardware and software by building scalable, efficient, and reliable platforms that support modern application delivery.

Since then, it's been so much fun that I keep learning and learning. I have worked in a variety of technical roles providing support, technical consultancy, and automation. I have a passion for cloud-native technologies and experience in Kubernetes, containers, IaC, service mesh, cloud, and recently, CNAPP.

I'm a strong proponent of continuous education and it's my strong conviction that no matter how experienced you are, there is always more to learn.

I started planning, assessing and building cloud computing in my company since the end of 2019 by conducting several cloud technology assessment tenders and selecting hyperscalers (cloud provider) according to my company's use cases. our challenge is how we maximize the benefits of cloud computing by migrating monolithic applications to microservices from on-premises to cloud using hyperscaler, with microservices we will have agile delivery to support rapid Go to Market (GTM) from a business point of view, we can support multiple deployments or sprint releases according to the business requirements program every week, it is much easier to maintain our applications / services independently and much more. Therefore, we realize the need to have microservices knowledge by understanding in-depth knowledge of the Kubernetes Platform to support this so that we will have an agile environment that can support our business growth and provide added value and competitive advantage for digital transformation in our company. I started learning Kubernetes from 2022 until now to support all kinds of applications/services migrating to microservices in the cloud. by fully understanding this knowledge we can maximize the value of kubernetes to support our business in our company.

His extensive knowledge is complemented by a range of industry-recognized certifications, including OSCP, OSEP, GCFA, GDAT, GSOM, Kubestronaut (CKA, CKS), and others.  Jakob's contributions to open-source initiatives have garnered recognition from SANS Institute, a leading authority in cybersecurity research and training. His Sigma rules were referenced by SANS ICS, while his co-authored open-source project (ATC RE&CT) was featured in the SANS MGT551 course.  Jakob brings a unique blend of technical expertise and passion for cybersecurity to his work. With a strong educational background in computer science and cybersecurity, Jakob has developed a deep understanding of complex security systems and threats. His dedication to staying at the forefront of industry trends and advancements has earned him a reputation as a knowledgeable and effective cybersecurity expert.  Jakob aspires to achieve a pioneering role as the first hacker-CISO within an S&P 500 organization, leveraging his expertise and passion for advancing cybersecurity best practices.

Working with the TAG Contributor Strategy Mentoring Working Group, he helps CNCF projects prepare for mentorship programs such as LFX Mentorship, Google Summer of Code, and Outreachy. His published paper, which received the Best Paper Award at IEEE SOLI 2021, can be accessed at: "ASIST: Automated Strategic Partners Ranking and Recommendation System Using NLP and Security Intelligence," 2021 IEEE International Conference on Service Operations and Logistics, and Informatics (SOLI), 2021 (https://ieeexplore.ieee.org/document/9672369).

I’ve worked on projects involving Grafana, InfluxDB, Docker, and cloud infrastructure across GCP and Azure. I’ve contributed to initiatives that improve system monitoring, security, and deployment automation. I’m passionate about cloud-native technologies and love sharing knowledge through meetups, talks, and hands-on workshops.

person.name: Seonyeong Park
person.bio: Cloud Infrastructure, Kubernetes, DevOps, MLOps, AIOps, SRE

person.name: Nicklas Risan Dahl
person.bio: Infrastructure engineer from Norway with a masters degree in cloud-based services and operations. Navigating the CNCF ecosystem has been a blast, and I'm looking forward to learn even more for years to come!

person.name: Tho Tran
person.bio: A lifelong learner driven by strong curiosity, I deeply admire the engineers and architects who build cutting-edge technology and am committed to achieving the mastery required to join their solutions.

person.name: Tobias Polley
person.bio: Trainer, Coach and Architect at predic8 GmbH (Bonn, Germany). Co-Host of the Bonner Microservices Meetup.

person.name: Amine Ben Mansour
person.bio: Passionate Cloud & SysOps Engineer and open-source advocate, specializing in building highly available, resilient, and secure architectures using cutting-edge declarative and automated approaches with tools from the CNCF ecosystem. Experienced with both on-premises data centers and public cloud environments, and AWS certified.  I am a lifelong learner who enjoys exchanging ideas and collaborating with like-minded professionals deeply interested in hands-on experimentation through personal side projects and a continuously evolving homelab :)

person.name: Davit Harutyunyan
person.bio: Davit Harutyunyan earned his B.Sc. and M.Sc. degrees with honors in Telecommunication Engineering from the National Polytechnic University of Armenia in 2011 and 2015, respectively. He completed his Ph.D. with honors in Information and Communication Technology at the University of Trento, Italy, in 2019. Alongside his academic pursuits, he worked as a Research Engineer in the Smart Networks and Services (SENSE) group at the FBK Research Institute in Italy from 2015 to 2020, and since 2021, he has been contributing as a Research Engineer in Corporate Research at Robert Bosch GmbH in Germany.  His primary research interests focus on advancing 5G/6G mobile networks, Open RAN, Network Function Virtualization (NFV), network automation, management, and orchestration. He has extensive experience in edge and cloud computing, particularly in leveraging lightweight virtualization technologies such as containers and their orchestration. He is also proficient in using DevOps tools and public cloud services for efficient and scalable infrastructure deployment.

person.name: Gerard Louie Pontino
person.bio: Platform Engineer with a strong focus on Kubernetes, cloud technologies, and modern DevOps practices. I specialize in building scalable, reliable systems, automating infrastructure, and streamlining deployments. With hands-on experience across development and operations, I design and manage platforms that are fast to deploy, secure by design, and built to perform in production-grade environments.

person.name: Krisztián Poós
person.bio: A highly skilled Architect with a solid background of 30 years experience in Linux systems and 19 years experience in Solaris systems operation and engineering and also 7 years experience in the cloud native field. A person who is not only focusing on the job itself but can take the lead about nearly any kind of problems and with good problem solving skills leading the team towards the solution while always keeping an eye on the business/customer impacts of the issue. A professional who is always open to any new technologies and can improve his skills on the direction which is needed. A person who can give his knowledge to the colleagues with holding trainings on several topics on unix/linux, security or cloud native fields. A person who believes in the lifelong learning both in the technical fields and the personal evolving as well.

person.name: Yifan Wang

I previously worked with OpenShift, which marked the beginning of my journey into the Kubernetes tech stack. This is now my fifth year in this field, and I sincerely hope Kubernetes continues to grow and thrive.

person.name: Armon Hakimi
person.bio: certified geek, navigating the Kubernetes cosmos with a smile:)) excited about venturing boldly where no pod has dared to go before🚀🧑‍🚀🤩  ...mainly in it for the jacket🫠

His contributions support better cloud-native decision-making, and he’s been an active speaker at KCD and KubeCon events.

person.name: Donghwan Won

I’m particularly passionate about exploring how these technologies drive innovation in the cloud ecosystem. For more insights, best practices, and deep dives into cloud-native development, check out my tech blog at showinfo8.com.

person.name: Michael Levan
person.bio: Michael Levan is a seasoned engineer and consultant in the Kubernetes and Platform Engineering space who spends his time working with startups and enterprises around the globe on consulting, training, and content creation. He is a trainer, 3x published author, podcast host, international public speaker, and was part of the Kubernetes v1.28 Release Team.Why I am excited to be an ambassador: The avenue that's now opened to work with more people around the world. I've been giving back to the community, creating free content, and contributing to projects for 5+ years. I'm now excited to work with others who are passionate about doing the same.

person.name: Mohamed Chaaben

Certified CNCF Kubestronaut with extensive experience designing, securing, and operating Kubernetes platforms (AKS, EKS, and self-managed). I am obsessed with home lab and like to tinker and learn new technologies.  Blog link: https://joysonfernandes.medium.com/  portfolio link: https://joysonfernandes.com/

person.name: Martin Bergamasco
person.bio: DevOps engineer passionate about real-time high throughput systems running on Kubernetes. Worked with multiple "big clouds" such as AWS and Azure, from system design to supporting production systems. Interested in everything IT (cloud or on-prem)

person.name: Sunil Hari

With a strong foundation in Cloud Native Technologies, I specialize in integrating security into DevOps practices to ensure robust, efficient, and compliant systems. My journey is driven by continuous learning, and I take pride in combining technical skills with a collaborative approach to solve complex challenges and deliver impactful results.

person.name: Jairus Christensen
person.bio: Jairus is a DevOps engineer with expertise in Kubernetes, security engineering, and AWS. He holds a degree in Cybersecurity with a minor in Computer Science from Brigham Young University. Jairus has contributed to open-source projects like OpenSSL and Nginx and maintains a project called ClusterCreator, a tool to run secure Kubernetes on Proxmox. With experience in bare-metal and cloud environments, he specializes in creating secure, scalable systems and enjoys experimenting with his homelab to simulate enterprise IT environments.

person.name: Shu Muto
person.bio: Shu Muto is a Principal Software Engineer at NEC Solution Innovators, Ltd., a company within the NEC group.He serves as one of the maintainers for Kubernetes Dashboard and holds the position of chair for SIG UI.Since 2019, Shu has been organizing Kubernetes Upstream Training sessions in Japan, dedicated to training both contributors and mentors, and to promote the culture of open source.Kubernetes Upstream Training in JapanKubernetes Upstream Training in remoteThe following presentations were given in relation to Kubernetes Upstream Training:[KCSNA2019] How to Hold Upstream Training in Your Country and Get New Contributors! - Shu Muto, Kenichi Oomichi, Akihito Inoh[KCSNA2022] How Kubernetes upstream training has been held in Japan for four years, getting over the COVID-19 situation - Shu Muto, Xie Ziyi[KubeDay Japan 2024] Keynote: Empowering Japanese Contributors: Kubernetes Upstream Training - Shu Muto, Ziyi XieIn addition, he serves as an organizer for the CNCF chapter in Japan, CNCF Cloud Native Community Japan, which was launched in November 2023.He hopes to spread the culture of development within the open source community more than ever before and promote collaboration among developers around the world.

person.name: Suman Chakraborty
person.bio: Suman Chakraborty is a Senior Cloud Native Architect with VMware by Broadcom. Suman helps customers in their application modernization journey using Cloud Native technologies and DevOps best practises. He has been a strong advocate of Open Source technology and a community person where he speaks on Kubernetes, Containers, Serverless and other topics within the CNCF ecosystem. Suman has been a seasoned International Speaker in Open Source Summit (EU), Devops India Summit, AWS User Group, Docker Community Event, HashiTalks, KCD Pune over past few years. AWS UserGroup - https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=0uBIvzydL1c&t=8s Knative event - https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=k0NXFr5iEFE Hasitalks - https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=qr_f85EuslQ&t=1s

Committed to standardization and programmable automation of cloud and cloud-native infrastructure, active practice devops, CI/CD and platform engineering based on Kubernetes and its ecology.

person.name: Renan Rodrigues

Projects such as Prometheus, CNI, Envoy, Cilium, and Flux are among my areas of expertise.

person.name: Anoop Jayadharan

person.name: Jonathan Bowe
person.bio: I'm a Senior Site Reliability Engineer, and I'm passionate about developing reliable systems that are simple to interact with and maintain. person.name: Roxane Gall

person.name: Marnik Van Hileghem
