person.name: Alessandro Quadrini

I am extremely passionate about the DevSecOps approach, GitOps, and the whole Cloud Native world. I love discovering each product under the Cloud Native Computing Foundation (CNCF) and applying them in the best possible scenarios. I love participating in the community! See you around.

person.name: Daniel Mccracken
person.bio: I am a DevOps and business professional with over two decades of experience spanning enterprise healthcare IT, application development, CI/CD, and Kubernetes administration. I helped found the Google Developers Group of Grand Rapids, MI, which sparked my interest in containerization and orchestration at scale. In 2019, I left the corporate world to help start DevsOperative, Inc. with my friends. They introduced me to Kubernetes around the time of the Kubernetes 1.0 launch, which inspired me to learn and invest in this amazing platform and community. My favorite tools are Helm, ArgoCD, and Cert Manager. Reach out to me on LinkedIn and checkout our stream on Twitch or YouTube called the Cloud Native Coffee Hour!

person.name: Fabio Pinna Lima
person.bio: Cloud and Dev[Sec]Ops Architect with extensive experience in Kubernetes, GitOps, and cloud-native infrastructure. He has worked with CNCF technologies across AWS, Azure, GCP, and OpenStack focusing on platform automation, security, and reliability. Fabio previously helped organize OpenStack Day Brazil and has contributed to community knowledge-sharing around DevOps and cloud adoption.

person.name: Frederico Muñoz
person.bio: Frederico started working with GNU and Linux in the mid-90s out of sheer curiosity, a decision that strongly influenced his life. Actively involved in the free software and open source community from early on, he made a path from programming and system administration towards data science, cloud computing and enterprise architecture. In recent years he has been actively involved in projects touching such diverse areas as Quantum computing, IoT, and analytics, and actively participating in the Kubernetes project as part of the Release Team, SIG Contribex, and others. He currently leads the Cloud and Architecture domain at SAS, for the South West & East Europe region. He holds a BA in Archaeology from the University of Lisbon, where he is an MA student and where he also took Physics.Why I am excited to be an ambassador: I see being a CNCF Ambassador as a great opportunity to engage with others both in the technical aspects of cloud-native computing and in the many ways that those interested can become active members of the community. Cloud-native technology has largely been developed through open collaboration, and being an Ambassador is taking a step towards more visible participation in building the community - and learning from it.

person.name: Mathijs Hoogland
person.bio: I'm a freelance DevOps engineer with a specialisation in Kubernetes and cloud native environments. I love helping companies transform technically and culturally to fully grasp the advantages of Kubernetes. Usually the tech is easy, it's about people.

person.name: Pete Wall

With a strong focus on developer experience and system reliability, I actively contribute to open-source projects and share solutions to real-world problems through my GitHub. I thrive in fast-paced environments where I can apply modern engineering principles to solve complex challenges in infrastructure and backend development.

My focus: stable foundations, faster delivery, and smarter spend.

person.name: James King

Designs and builds Cloud Native solutions for a range of clients, blending technical knowledge with an ability to explain clearly and engage audiences.

person.name: Lukáš Šabľa
person.bio: Kubernetes wasn’t an instant love affair, but over time, it became a passion. I don’t believe everything is a nail—but Kubernetes is a really good hammer. I’ve been building and managing platforms since the days of PHP 4.3, mastering Linux, DevOps, and Kubernetes along the way. My journey took me through years at Slovakia’s largest hosting company, where I tackled real-world infrastructure challenges at scale. Now, with many years of experience, I design fault-tolerant infrastructures for high-traffic media, e-commerce, and on-demand video platforms, ensuring they stay reliable, scalable, and ready for whatever comes next.

person.name: Tanguy Nicolas
person.bio: DevOps engineer, specializing in Cloud Computing. Enthused by Linux and Cloud Native technologies. Regular user of Helm, Terraform, Argo CD, Cilium, ...

person.name: Joost Evertse
person.bio: Joost Evertse is a Kubernetes, Terraform, and Ansible expert with a lot of hands-on experience in cloud-native and CI/CD technologies. Besides that 25 years of experience in designing, implementing, and managing IT solutions across industries including finance, telecom, energy, and e-commerce. Personal blog on http://blog.joustie and professional site http://www.hocmodo.nl.

person.name: Martin Weber
person.bio: As a Technical Lead and Platform Architect at Axel Springer National Media & Tech GmbH & Co. KG, I am responsible for designing, developing, and maintaining cloud-native platforms and solutions for the media and tech division of the leading European digital publisher. With over 15 years of experience in software engineering, DevOps, and cloud computing, I have acquired multiple CNCF certifications and extensive skills in AWS, Kubernetes and Terraform, among other technologies. I am passionate about delivering high-quality and reliable solutions that enable the digital transformation and innovation of Axel Springer and its brands. I am also committed to sharing my knowledge and expertise with my colleagues and the wider community, as well as learning from others and embracing new challenges. I am looking for opportunities to collaborate with diverse and talented teams and to contribute to the mission and vision of Axel Springer.
person.company: Axel Springer National Media and Tech GmbH & Co. KG

person.name: Pritesh Bhavsar
person.bio: I am a Software Engineer with over 3 years of experience, specializing in Kubernetes, AWS, and cloud-native technologies. Passionate about automation and infrastructure as code. While I actively engage in the cloud-native community
